title: Conclave at Deneba
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Update|[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]}} {{Event |prev= |conc= |next= |image=[[File:Conclave_on_Deneba.jpg]] |name=Conclave at Deneba |conflict=[[Krath Holy Crusade]][[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 3|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 3]] |date=[[3997 BBY]] |place=[[Mount Meru (Deneba)|Mount Meru]]; [[Deneba/Legends|Deneba]][[Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith'' audio drama]] |outcome=Pyrrhic Jedi victory; [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] leaves to infiltrate the [[Krath/Legends|Krath]] |keyparties=[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] |participants=*[[Vodo-Siosk Baas]] *[[Shoaneb Culu]] *[[Crado]] *[[Dace Diath]] *[[Tott Doneeta]] *[[Arca Jeth]]† *[[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr]] *[[Cay Qel-Droma]] *[[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] *[[Shayoto]] *[[Nomi Sunrider]] *[[Sylvar]] *[[Thon]] *[[Sli'Lon Tahar]]{{CasusBelliCite|115|Le facteur X}} *Unidentified number of unnamed [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] }} {{Quote|The Jedi are disturbed at how quickly Satal and Aleema have mastered the Sith powers. They've called an assembly of all the Jedi in the galaxy. We're on our way to Deneba.|Nomi Sunrider|audio=ConclaveOnDeneba1-DLOTS.flac|}} The '''Conclave at Deneba''', also known as the '''Grand Assembly''', '''Jedi Assembly''', or the '''Great Council of Deneba''', was a [[Jedi Convocation]] that took place during the [[Krath Holy Crusade]], held on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Deneba/Legends|Deneba]] in [[3997 BBY]]. The gathering of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] was called in response to the growing threat of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] that was previously thought to have been eliminated on [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] during the [[Freedon Nadd Uprising]] a [[3998 BBY|year earlier]]. Nearly ten thousand in number, the Jedi gathered in an amphitheater in the valley at the foot of Deneba's [[Mount Meru (Deneba)|Mount Meru]], where the spokesman for the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]], a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] named [[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr]], spoke of an ancient [[Dark Lord prophecy|prophecy]] that stated that the legendary [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lords]] of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] would return to the forefront of [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] events. Many other Jedi spoke at the event, including [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] who brought forward the controversial idea of [[Mission to Empress Teta|infiltrating]] the Sith-inspired Krath in order to destroy them from within. Qel-Droma's plan was immediately met with scorn from many of the Jedi present, including his former Master [[Arca Jeth]], as well as the aged Jedi Master [[Shayoto]]. The conclave was then brought to recess, allowing many of the later arrivals to situate themselves as the meeting began again. One such latecomer was the [[Krevaaki]] Master [[Vodo-Siosk Baas]], who arrived just as a [[Battle of Deneba|surprise attack]], initiated by the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]-inspired [[Krath/Legends|Krath]], interrupted the assembly with a bombardment of life-pods. The pods contained deadly [[Krath war droid/Legends|Krath war droids]], which, upon release, began to attack the assembly. The ensuing battle resulted in the deaths of many Jedi, including the [[Arkanian/Legends|Arkanian]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Arca Jeth]], as well as further influencing Ulic's choice of infiltration. In spite of the numerous warnings against such action, Ulic indeed pursued the Krath, to their home of [[Empress Teta/Legends|Koros Major]]. ==Background== ===Trouble in Empress Teta=== {{Quote|Master Arca…Despite all the appeals of the Galactic Senate, the brutal atrocities continue in the Empress Teta system…''"
"''Yes…The Krath have unleashed the dark side with ''great'' malevolence.|Ulic Qel-Droma and [[Arca Jeth]]|}} [[File:JfleetDeneba.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Thousands of Jedi ships descend upon the planet [[Deneba/Legends|Deneba]].]] In [[3998 BBY]], [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Arca Jeth]] led a team of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] and fought a series of battles on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]], known as the [[Freedon Nadd Uprising]].''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising]]'' [[Freedon Nadd]], an ancient [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], had held Onderon under the sway of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] for nearly four centuries, until the arrival of Master Jeth, when Jeth banished Nadd's spirit and liberated the Onderonian people of his dark influence. In doing so, the Jedi believed that the dark side had been kept in check.[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 1|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 1]] In [[3997 BBY]], the Jedi learned that the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Empress Teta system]] had suffered a violent [[Political uprising of the Empress Teta system|political uprising]], staged by the heirs to the thrones of [[Empress Teta/Legends|Empress Teta]], Satal and [[Aleema Keto]]. The Ketos had also been on Onderon during the last [[Second Battle of Onderon|battle]] of the Uprising, and had acquired knowledge of ancient [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]]. Newly armed with their sorcery, they established the [[Krath/Legends|Krath]], a secret society of [[Sith sorcerer]]s. To solidify their rule over the system, the Krath laid siege to their own homeworld of Empress Teta, which was the last of the [[Tetan/Legends|Tetan]] worlds to resist the coup.[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 2|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 2]] Master Jeth was suspicious that the unrest in Empress Teta was somehow connected to the uprising on Onderon, so he and his Knights accepted [[Jedi Watchman/Legends|guardianship]] of the Tetan system. The Jedi then [[Battle of Koros Major|engaged]] the Krath, with assistance from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], intending to restoring peace in Tetan space. However, when the Krath manifested their Sith powers, the joint Jedi/Republic effort was bewildered and defeated. Considering how briskly the Krath managed to spread the influence of the dark side across the galaxy, the leaders of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] became increasingly concerned that an ancient [[Dark Lord prophecy|prophecy]] which stated that the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lords]] of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] would rise again was on the verge of coming to fruition. The result was something that had not happened in hundreds of years: a gathered assembly of all Jedi was called. ===Arrival on Deneba=== {{Quote|I have lived for many, many years…and this is the first time, that all the Jedi have gathered in one place.''"
"''Master, are all the Jedi in the galaxy really here?''"
"''All but a few.|Arca Jeth and Ulic Qel-Droma {{Audio|ConclaveOnDenebaArrival-DLOTS.flac|Listen}}|}} [[File:ThonArca.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Masters Thon and Arca, discussing their comrade Vodo-Siosk Baas.]] Because the dark side played an integral part in the Krath's rise to power, the Jedi Masters decided to meet and discuss whether the situation warranted a renewed Jedi war effort. Almost every Master, Knight, and [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] in the galaxy was summoned to Deneba, numbering close to ten thousand in total.{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/sourcearchive/news20080111.html|text=Lost For Over 300 Years|int=Dark Empire endnotes}} Among those present were Master Arca Jeth and [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]], [[Nomi Sunrider]] along with her daughter [[Vima Sunrider|Vima]], and the [[Tchuukthai]] Jedi [[Thon]]. Sunrider and Qel-Droma had returned from a recent failed relief mission to Empress Teta where, in an attempt to drive the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] out of Tetan space, real Krath [[CX-133 Chaos fighter|CX-133 Chaos]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] began making suicide attack runs in the midst of illusory ones. Qel-Droma was injured during the assault, and the Republic fleet was forced to retreat and rethink their battle strategy. The Jedi began to discuss the battle that Cay Qel-Droma had missed, but, as the conference was about to begin, they proceeded to take their places in the amphitheater. ==The conclave== ===Message to the conclave=== {{Dialogue|Ulic|By the Force! How many Jedi are here?|Arca|Thousands. The Jedi have guarded the Republic for centuries, and we have grown strong.|Cay|And I'll bet we'll continue to grow strong for centuries to come. What could ever bring the Jedi down?''"
'''Arca''': "''That remains to be seen, Cay. But quiet now. Prepare yourselves. The spokesman for the Jedi is a Master named Odan-Urr. He is a telepath; and if you've never experienced his form of communication, the first time can be…disconcerting.|attr=Ulic, Cay, and Master Arca {{Audio|ConclaveOnDenebaMessage-DLOTS.flac|Listen}}|src=}} [[File:TelepathicTalk.jpg|thumb|left|280px|Odan-Urr, speaking telepathically to the masses through the Force.]] The convocation of Jedi gathered in a massive hollowed-out valley at the center of [[Mount Meru (Deneba)|Mount Meru]], with a great stage in the center of the valley. Recognized as one of the oldest and wisest of the Jedi, the [[Draethos/Legends|Draethos]] Jedi Master [[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr]] sat on that stage, and as the spokesman for the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Council]], Odan-Urr was also chosen to preside over the convention. Just as the assemblage was beginning, Master Jeth cautioned Ulic and the others about the Draethos, [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] that used telepathy as their natural means of speech. Before he could finish, their minds were suddenly filled with the voice of Odan-Urr. Though unnerved at first, the Jedi relaxed and listened intently as Odan-Urr recounted the prophecy of the return of the Dark Lords, and also of the catastrophe at Empress Teta. The question he posed was whether the Jedi should send a mightier force to deal with the dark-side threat, just as was done with Onderon. It was at this time Ulic Qel-Droma approached the stage. ====Ulic's plan==== {{Quote|The darksiders act in ''complete secrecy'', until the leaders of society have fallen to their evil seductions. Then they strike with great suddenness, bringing down civilizations it took ''aeons'' to build.|Master Arca, warning Ulic Qel-Droma about the wiles of darksiders|audio=ConclaveOnDenebaArcaWarnsUlic-DLotS.flac|}} [[File:DaceCayQrrrlDeneba.jpg|thumb|right|120px|Dace Diath, Cay Qel-Droma, and Qrrrl Toq at the Conclave on Deneba.]] Qel-Droma was acknowledged by the Draethos Jedi, and through the Force, Odan-Urr delivered Ulic's words to the minds of all the attendees. He began by discussing the past events, and how the wrong approach had been taken in dealing with the Krath. Qel-Droma felt that the Jedi were mistaken, that overwhelming military strength would never be capable of completely eliminating the dark side. He believed that even if the dark side were defeated in Tetan space, there was nothing preventing it from taking root elsewhere in the galaxy and spreading again. However, Qel-Droma's history of success in battle was questioned amongst his associates; he had just returned from a defeat, and many Jedi were unsure if he was capable of leading them to any sort of victory. He then unfurled his true plan: not one of direct confrontation, but infiltration. Qel-Droma proposed that a Jedi be chosen to effectively become a double-agent, feigning allegiance to the Krath while secretly learning their ways and plotting to destroy them. The assembly was in an uproar; Qel-Droma was proposing the unthinkable. Many of them, including Master Jeth, advised him against such foolishness, saying that the only way to defeat the dark side was to face it down. Knights like [[Shoaneb Culu]] and [[Dace Diath]], who [[Second Battle of Onderon|fought]] with Ulic on Onderon, also spoke against his plan. The aged Master Shayoto spoke of the dangers of attempting to destroy the dark side from within. Master Jeth agreed with Shayoto, and warned Qel-Droma that he could not destroy the dark side entirely, but merely attempt to [[Force Light|subdue]] it as Thon when he trapped the darkness of the planet [[Ambria/Legends|Ambria]] inside of a [[Lake Natth|lake]] on that world. Jeth continued, stating that even though the Jedi could never hope to destroy the dark side, through their combined efforts they could create a [[wall of light]] to drive the dark side away. A recess was then called, as several other Jedi arrived on Deneba. ===The shadow of Freedon Nadd=== {{Dialogue|Exar Kun|Master Vodo! Master Vodo!|Arca|Did you feel that?—|Vodo|Oh, Exar Kun…it is as I predicted. Darkness has found you!|Arca|You must help him, Vodo!|Vodo|Yes…I will meditate in the Force, try to draw it's power around him…Exar Kun. Exar Kun!|attr=Arca Jeth and Vodo-Siosk Baas, feeling Exar Kun's distress through the Force {{Audio|ConclaveOnDenebaShadowofNadd-DLOTS.flac|Listen}}|src=}} [[File:PsychicVodo.jpg|thumb|left|260px|[[Vodo-Siosk Baas]] senses [[Freedon Nadd]]'s dark spirit around his apprentice, light years across the galaxy.]] Only a small number of Jedi were absent from Deneba, and during the recess many of those arrived late, as the distance they traveled was so great. Among the tardy was Master [[Vodo-Siosk Baas]]. The [[Krevaaki]] Jedi was in terrible dismay; his greatest student, [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]], had left him in search of dark-side knowledge, and this worried Vodo tremendously. He knew that somehow Exar Kun would be swept up in the dark times ahead. Vodo was looking for Master Jeth as he came across Ulic and Cay Qel-Droma, just as Cay was admonishing Ulic for his bold proposal. After a brief exchange, Vodo was pointed in Arca Jeth's direction. Greeting each other, the two Masters walked abreast while discussing Exar Kun and his fascination with the dark side. Suddenly, both Vodo and Jeth felt a violent call through the Force, and Vodo instantly recognized it as his apprentice Exar Kun. Unknown to him or any of the other Jedi who heard the call, Exar had been severely hurt by the dark side by the [[Force ghost/Legends|dark spirit]] of [[Freedon Nadd]], who was tempting him to join the dark side in order to avoid death. At Master Jeth's behest and Kun's cries for help from his master, Vodo reached out with the Force and meditated around his student. When Nadd's spirit realized that Exar Kun was receiving outside assistance, he set his attention on Vodo. Vodo sensed it too late, just as Nadd lashed out at him through the Force. He was knocked back, and his connection to Exar Kun was lost. Knowing there was nothing more he could do for his student, Vodo and the other Jedi took their places in the arena, as the convocation was about to resume. ===Battle of Deneba=== {{Main|Battle of Deneba}} {{Quote|Assimilating Jedi battle tactics.''"
"''Assimilate this!|Krath war droid before being destroyed by Cay Qel-Droma|audio=AssimilateThis-DLOTS.flac|}} Before the conclave could resume, the Jedi were ambushed by hundreds of [[Escape pod/Legends|life-pods]] that fell from orbit to Deneba's surface, unleashing a deadly cargo of [[Krath war droid/Legends|Krath war droids]]. When the pods opened to reveal the droids, the unsuspecting Jedi became quickly embroiled in a fierce battle. Master Jeth quickly suggested that they erect a protective [[Force barrier/Legends|shield wall]] with the Force, but Cay Qel-Droma noted that it was too late. He then instructed his [[T-12]] servant droid to investigate the area, but upon receipt of the order the droid's programming changed, and all of the servant droids received new instructions to eliminate the Jedi. The Krath droids put up a formidable offensive, but despite their intense assault the Jedi emerged victorious. In the heat of the battle, however, Master Arca Jeth was killed by a war droid. ==Outcome== {{Quote|Your Master is gone…what will you do now, Ulic?''"
"''Nomi…I will do what I said I would do. I will destroy the Krath from within.|Ulic Qel-Droma and Nomi Sunrider, shortly before he departs for Cinnagar|}} [[File:HuggingBrothers.jpg|thumb|right|220px|The Qel-Droma brothers embrace shortly before Ulic departs for the Empress Teta system.]] After the battle, the day's events were recorded into the [[Tedryn Holocron|personal]] [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] of Master Vodo-Siosk Baas. He recounted the travesty of Arca's [[Death/Legends|death]], and also mentioned the discovery of the Krath being responsible for the war-droid attack. Meanwhile, Ulic Qel-Droma sat with Master Jeth's robes, while suffering feelings of incredible guilt for his Master's death. He vowed to carry out his plan, despite the warnings of the Masters. Sunrider implored him to change his mind, saying that an idea like that would only result in calamity, but Qel-Droma was unmoved. Cay tried also to sway his brother from making desperate decisions like this one, but he too was unsuccessful. The Jedi returned to Ossus, where Qel-Droma and Sunrider sought more information regarding the Empress Teta system. Qel-Droma spent the next days learning everything possible about the Krath and their home system, in order to develop a better understanding of what he was dealing with. Traveling to the [[Great Jedi Library/Legends|Great Jedi Library]], he and Sunrider were greeted by Master [[Ood Bnar]]. Bnar provided Qel-Droma with a comprehensive history of Empress Teta, but also warned him about his rash decision. Before leaving, Bnar brought the Knights before Master Odan-Urr, the Jedi [[Keeper of Antiquities]]. Odan-Urr then revealed his rare [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocron]], and displayed for Sunrider and Qel-Droma stories of ancient Jedi who fell to the dark side. However, the young Jedi Qel-Droma was unmoved. He stated that he was not seeking the Masters' approval, but only their understanding. The Masters recognized his determination, but also knew that attempting to hinder him was folly. Ood Bnar and Odan-Urr then departed, offering Qel-Droma their blessing.[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 4|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 4]] Before leaving Ossus, Ulic Qel-Droma had a conversation with his brother Cay. Cay adamantly believed that his brother was making a foolish decision, and was nervous that he would never see him again. All his attempts to sway his brother were naught, as Ulic's determination remained unwavering. The two brothers embraced one another as Ulic assured Cay that everything would turn out all right. Ulic then spent the next morning with Nomi Sunrider, preparing himself and his [[Starship/Legends|starship]] for departure to the Tetan system. Refusing repeated offers of assistance from Sunrider, he left her with a passionate kiss and departed for Empress Teta. The Jedi would not hold another convocation until the [[Conclave on Exis Station|Conclave of Exis Station]] in [[3986 BBY]].''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' ==Principal participants== ===Odan-Urr=== {{Main|Odan-Urr/Legends}} [[File:OdanUrrDeneba-DLotS3.jpg|left|170px]] Trained by the ancient [[Celegian/Legends|Celegian]] Master [[Ooroo/Legends|Ooroo]],''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith]]'' Odan-Urr himself grew into one of the oldest and wisest Jedi of the Order. As a young Padawan he was an avid learner, relentlessly studying Jedi lore and history, and learning ancient and forgotten [[Force power/Legends|Force techniques]]. He played an influential role in the [[Unification Wars (Koros)/Legends|Unification Wars]] of the Empress Teta system, displaying his proficiency in the rare art of Jedi [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]]. During this time, he became a personal advisor of [[Empress/Legends|Empress]] [[Teta/Legends|Teta]] herself. In [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], a prophetic vision forewarned Odan-Urr of an impending invasion from the fabled [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]], which lead to the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]]. As a Knight Odan-Urr again went to battle, alongside his warrior Empress and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. After the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] were defeated, Odan-Urr found a Sith holocron in the spoils of one of the abandoned Sith warship. In the wake of the war, Odan-Urr founded the Great Jedi Library on Ossus, where he kept his prized Sith holocron among other antiquities.''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire]]'' Odan-Urr eventually attained the rank of Master, and by the time of the conclave on Deneba, Odan-Urr had presided over Jedi assemblies for nearly six centuries. He was the acting spokesman for the Jedi Council. ===Ulic Qel-Droma=== {{Main|Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends}} [[File:DenebaUlic.jpg|left|130px]] Ulic Qel-Droma was a famed Jedi Knight and, along with his brother Cay, was the apprentice of Arca Jeth. During his time as Master Arca's padawan, Qel-Droma was assigned to a diplomatic mission to the planet Onderon. Together with Cay and the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] Jedi Tott Doneeta, he traveled to the dark-side world, where their mission utterly failed. The Jedi became involved in Onderon's [[Beast Wars|Beast War]] conflict, and assisted [[Beast-Lord]] [[Oron Kira]] in [[Battle of Iziz|defeating]] [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Amanoa]] and her dark-side legions.''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Ulic Qel-Droma and the Beast Wars of Onderon]]'' In the wake of the Beast Wars, Qel-Droma and his Jedi companions battled Amanoa's husband, the sorcerer [[King/Legends|King]] [[Ommin/Legends|Ommin]] and the dark-side spirit of Freedon Nadd. After emerging victoriously over King Ommin, Qel-Droma was one of several Jedi aware that the dark side had not been entirely defeated, and had made its way to the Empress Teta system. As such, Master Arca and his apprentices accepted watchman duties for Empress Teta. Arca then sent Qel-Droma and Nomi Sunrider as Jedi liaisons to the Galactic Republic, where they again requested assistance on behalf of the Tetan worlds. The Jedi-Republic effort was unsuccessful, and during the battle for Empress Teta, Qel-Droma was deeply wounded. As a result of the mission failure, the Jedi Order called a Jedi gathering to the planet Deneba, where Qel-Droma revealed his plan to secretly infiltrate the Krath. Despite the unanimous opposition of the Jedi assembly, Ulic Qel-Droma was intent on destroying the dark side from within. ===Arca Jeth=== {{Main|Arca Jeth}} [[File:DenebaArca.jpg|left|110px]] Arca Jeth of Arkania was the Jedi Master of Ulic and Cay Qel-Droma, as well as Twi'lek Tott Doneeta. During his time as Knight, Arca participated in the [[Hyabb-Twith Campaigns|Hyabb-Twith Campaign]] of [[4050 BBY]], a Jedi effort to rid the [[Hyabb-Twith Corridor]] of the infamous [[Nelori Marauders]].''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'' [[4015 BBY|Thirty-five years later]], he played a major role in the [[Great Droid Revolution]], where he revealed a little known Force power that allowed a Jedi to [[Ionize|disable]] a droid's internal mechanisms with the Force. As the watchman of the [[Japrael system/Legends|Onderon system]], Arca Jeth sent his three Padawans in his place to mediate the civil instability of the system's namesake planet. When his apprentices proved unsuccessful, Master Arca intervened, using the power of Jedi battle meditation to soundly defeat Queen Amanoa and her dark-side army. Arca and his apprentices battled the dark side again, this time in the form of Amanoa's husband King Ommin, and the spirit of Sith Lord Freedon Nadd. Arca and his Jedi eventually triumphed over Ommin, and managed to cleanse Onderon of the darkness. Arca was also aware that Sith magic had escaped Onderon, and taken root in the Empress Teta system. When the Jedi were called en masse to Deneba for a discussion of the darkness in the Tetan system, Arca was adamant in his stance against Ulic's plan to ally with the Krath. ===Shayoto=== {{Main|Shayoto}} [[File:Shayoto.jpg|left|130px]] Shayoto was an aged Jedi Master, who lived though the times of such galactic conflicts as the [[Gank Massacres]] of [[4800 BBY]], and the [[Vultar Cataclysm]] nearly [[4250 BBY|five hundred years later]]. Shayoto was also very familiar with the machinations of the Sith, and advocated fervently against Ulic Qel-Droma's intentions of joining the Krath. After being overlooked and nearly ignored by the assembly of Jedi, Shayoto was finally allowed to speak, and was given the floor by Arca Jeth. He then recounted such previous conflicts as the Great Hyperspace War, and spoke of how his ''Master's'' Master told him of the [[Hundred-Year Darkness/Legends|Hundred-Year Darkness]]. From this ancient Jedi, Shayoto learned how the darksiders implemented their evil schemes, acting in utter secrecy and only striking when their victims were most vulnerable. He warned Ulic and the assembly of Knights about the dangers and wiles of the dark side, and how in the past darksiders were successful in toppling entire civilizations. ==Behind the scenes== The Conclave at Deneba first appeared in the issue [[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 3|''Dark Lords of the Sith 3: Descent to the Dark Side'']], for the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'']] series of comics. The battle was conceived and written by authors [[Tom Veitch]] and [[Kevin J. Anderson]], and was drawn by artist [[Chris Gossett]]. Veitch and Anderson chose to use the conclave as the backdrop for the death of Arca Jeth, which itself was a pivotal moment from which to build on Ulic Qel-Droma's fall to the dark side. The Conclave at Deneba was also featured in the [[Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith'' audio drama]], with voice actors [[John Cygan]] (Ulic Qel-Droma), [[Glynnis Talken Campbell|Glynnis Talken]] (Nomi Sunrider), and [[Jim Ward (voice actor)|Jim Ward]] (Arca Jeth), adding a more detailed perspective on an already established story. The warning about darksiders given to Ulic by Master Arca in the audio adaptation was spoken by Master [[Shayoto]] in the comic version. ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' mistakenly states that Exar Kun slew Vodo-Siosk Baas during the Conclave on Deneba,[[Exar Kun/Legends|''Exar Kun's'']] entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' when in fact Kun killed him following the [[Battle of Coruscant (Great Sith War)|battle of Coruscant]].[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 2]] The [[Star Wars Insider 26|''Star Wars Insider'' 26]] article [[The Horse's Mouth|''Straight from the Horse's Mouth'']] places the Conclave at Deneba at [[3996 BBY]], rather than the established date of 3997 BBY. As ''Straight from the Horse's Mouth'' contradicts several other established dates,{{InsiderCite|26|The Horse's Mouth|Straight from the Horse's Mouth: A Guide to the ''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'' Universe, Part 1}} this article treats it as incorrect. ==Appearances== *[[Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith'' audio drama]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 3|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 3]] {{1st}} *[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 4|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 4]] {{Imo}} *[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 5|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 5]] {{Mo}} *{{KOTORbackups|19}} {{Imo}} *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' {{Codex}} {{1stID|Conclave at Deneba}} *''[[Champions of the Force]]'' {{1stm}} ===Non-canon appearances=== *{{CasusBelliCite|115|Le facteur X}} {{Un}} ==Sources== *{{InsiderCite|26|The Horse's Mouth|Straight from the Horse's Mouth: A Guide to the ''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'' Universe, Part 1}} {{Imo}} *''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *{{InsiderCite|27|The Horse's Mouth|Straight from the
